product_name: Analytical Methodology - eleven discipline pages from a Georgetown Applied Intelligence curriculum, treating each analytical method as its own working surface
representative_input: A reviewer opens the project surface and runs one bounded workflow from a sample input through an exportable or persisted result.
operation_under_test: Execute the workflow, preserve state, export or display the result, and record acceptance checks.
expected_output: A visible result record containing input summary, action trace, output artifact, error state if any, and claim boundary.
acceptance_tests: Input fixture is explicit and repeatable.
Operation under test is specific to this project, not generic portfolio prose.
Expected output is inspectable as a file, page state, table, packet, or export.
Acceptance criteria are observable and falsifiable.
Claim boundary prevents stronger claims than the evidence supports.
At least one load-bearing source role is verified or explicitly marked as awaiting human close reading.
The workflow records state or produces an exportable output.
falsifier: The fixture fails if the workflow cannot be repeated, if output is not inspectable, or if the page only describes a system without demonstrating the operation.
evidence_to_capture: reviewer or outside-use evidence before stronger public claims
current_gap_from_operational_standing: human-verified source integration still missing | full-build readiness score below expert release threshold | outside/user validation not yet recorded
next_build_step: Add a replayable demo with before/after state, failure mode, and exported result so the software utility is visible rather than asserted.
claim_boundary: This proof fixture is a build instrument. It does not by itself prove final operational standing until executed and captured.
